from pyspark.sql.functions import *

# ── 1. CONFIG
raw_path    = "abfss://ecommerce@tayyabadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/"
silver_path = "abfss://ecommerce@tayyabadls.dfs.core.windows.net/silver/"


if not file_name:
    raise ValueError("No file_name parameter received!")

# ── 3. READ
df_raw = spark.read \
    .format("csv") \
    .option("header", "true") \
    .option("inferSchema", "true") \
    .load(raw_path + file_name)

# ── 4. SELECT columns
df = df_raw.select(
    "user_id", "product_id", "category", "subcategory","brand", "price", "discount", "final_price",
    "rating", "review_count", "stock", "seller_id", "seller_rating","purchase_date", "shipping_time_days",
    "location", "device", "payment_method","is_returned", "delivery_status" )
    
# ── 5. TRANSFORM
df_clean = df \
    .withColumn("purchase_date",   to_date(col("purchase_date"))) \
    .withColumn("category",        trim(col("category"))) \
    .withColumn("delivery_status", trim(col("delivery_status"))) \
    .withColumn("ingested_at",     current_timestamp()) \
    .dropna(subset=["user_id", "product_id", "purchase_date"])

# print(f"Rows after cleaning: {df_clean.count()}")

# ── 6. WRITE Delta to Silver
df_clean.write \
    .format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("mergeSchema", "true") \
    .save(silver_path)
